records.ety: [F. rebelle, fr. L. rebellis. See Rebel, v. i.]

defs: Pertaining to rebels or rebellion; acting in revolt; rebellious; as, rebel troops.
quotes: Whoso be rebel to my judgment.
Convict by flight, and rebel to all law.

usage: -- Rebel, Insurgent. Insurgent marks an early, and rebel a more advanced, stage of opposition to government. The former rises up against his rulers, the latter makes war upon them.

records.ety: [F. rebeller, fr. L. rebellare to make war again; pref. re- again + bellare to make war, fr. bellum war. See Bellicose, and cf. Revel to carouse.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rebelled (r*bld); p. pr. & vb. n. Rebelling.]

defs: To renounce, and resist by force, the authority of the ruler or government to which one owes obedience. See Rebellion.
quotes: The murmur and the churls' rebelling.
Ye have builded you an altar, that ye might rebel this day against the Lord.

defs: To be disobedient to authority; to assume a hostile or insubordinate attitude; to revolt.
quotes: How could my hand rebel against my heart? How could your heart rebel against your reason?
